title: REC-2: Replace text pattern matching with tree-sitter AST scanning in forensic.ts + extend detector.ts
description: Replace inferPatternHint() text-based pattern matching with tree-sitter AST import analysis. Upgrade confidence scoring so web frameworks can reach HIGH confidence (currently capped at MEDIUM). Extend detector.ts to return structured tech profile with AST-level detection results. Add git churn weighted sampling for smarter file selection during forensic scan.

files.change: Replace text regex matching with AST import node traversal via web-tree-sitter. Upgrade confidence algorithm: >3 import statements from framework package = HIGH, 1-3 = MEDIUM, keyword-only = LOW. Set ast_level: true when AST parsing succeeded. Add optional git churn weighting: files with more recent git changes get higher priority in sample selection.

Extend detectFramework() return type to structured TechProfile: { framework: string; version?: string; confidence: 'HIGH' | 'MEDIUM' | 'LOW'; ast_evidence: string[]; co_packages: string[] }. This is re-exported from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node — may need shared package update or local type extension.

implementation: Import web-tree-sitter lazily at top of inferPatternHint() — load WASM once per process via module-level singleton
Parse candidate files with tree-sitter JavaScript/TypeScript grammar, extract all import declarations (ImportDeclaration nodes)
Build import source list, cross-reference against EXPECTED_CONFIG_FILES_BY_FRAMEWORK registry for framework identification
Implement confidence scoring: count import occurrences + config file presence + package.json deps → HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW threshold
Update PatternHintResult to set ast_level: true when AST parsed, retain text fallback when WASM unavailable
Add git churn helper: run `git log --follow --oneline -20 <file>` per candidate, weight selection by recency
Extend detector.ts TechProfile type and update detectFramework() return shape
Update callers of detectFramework() to handle new structured return (check forensic.ts usage of detector output)

reference.pattern: Lazy WASM loading singleton, text fallback pattern
reference.examples: PatternHintResult.ast_level boolean already exists — set it true when AST path taken

criteria: A React TypeScript file with 5+ React imports returns confidence=HIGH (previously MEDIUM)
PatternHintResult.ast_level is true when tree-sitter parsed the file
Text-based fallback still functions when WASM unavailable (ast_level=false, confidence capped at MEDIUM)
detectFramework() returns structured TechProfile with at minimum { framework, confidence, ast_evidence }
git churn weighting adjusts file selection order (top-churned files sampled first)
Existing forensic scan CLI output shape unchanged (PatternHintResult fields all present)

rationale.chosen_approach: AST import traversal is structurally precise — no false positives from comments or strings that text regex produces
decision_factors: PatternHintResult.ast_level boolean already exists in codebase
rationale.tradeoffs: WASM adds ~3.5MB to CLI package; git churn adds subprocess call overhead (acceptable for scan command, not hot path)

manual_checks: Run fab scan on a React TypeScript project and verify confidence=HIGH for React framework
Disable WASM (rename WASM file) and verify graceful fallback to text matching
Verify git churn sampling changes file order when git history differs
success_metrics: Web framework confidence reaches HIGH for projects with >3 framework import files
Scan command completes within 5 seconds on a 500-file project
Zero regressions in existing forensic report shape

risks.description: tree-sitter grammar parses TSX differently from TS — import nodes may have different AST shapes
risks.mitigation: Test ImportDeclaration extraction against both .ts and .tsx sample files before full implementation
risks.fallback: Use tree-sitter-typescript grammar which handles TSX via embedded language

risks.description: git churn subprocess calls fail in projects without git history
risks.mitigation: Wrap git command in try/catch; return neutral weight if git unavailable

functions.purpose: Module-level singleton that loads WASM once, returns cached parser
Parse source with tree-sitter, walk ImportDeclaration nodes, return source specifiers
functions.returns: Array of import source strings e.g. ['react', '@tanstack/react-query']

functions.purpose: Count framework-related imports and apply threshold: >=3 → HIGH, 1-2 → MEDIUM, 0 → LOW
